title: PyTorch features Miles, an open stack for large-scale LLM reinforcement learning
friendlyTitle: Miles helps teams train large AI models with reinforcement learning
summary: The PyTorch blog detailed Miles, RadixArk’s open-source framework for large-scale LLM reinforcement-learning post-training. Miles combines SGLang rollouts, Megatron-LM training, Ray orchestration, PyTorch extensibility, low-precision recipes, MoE alignment, observability, and fault tolerance.
friendlySummary: Miles is an open framework that connects several major AI infrastructure tools to make large-scale LLM post-training easier to run and customize.
whyItMatters: RL post-training is now a distributed-systems problem as much as a model-training problem. Open, composable stacks like Miles can help research and infrastructure teams reproduce advanced post-training workflows instead of relying only on closed lab methods.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This helps more teams experiment with the methods behind modern reasoning and agent models, instead of needing a giant private lab setup.
